tasks.design: Deliberately zero-new-infra: a fixed-window counter in a module-level Map, same spirit as the existing src/lib/apiCache.js NodeCache pattern but a separate concern (rate limiting, not response caching). This is a per-serverless-instance limiter — on Vercel each instance has its own Map, so it does NOT coordinate across instances (same gotcha as apiCache.js's per-instance NodeCache, documented in root CLAUDE.md's Architecture section). A true cross-instance guarantee would need a shared store (e.g. Upstash Redis via the Vercel Marketplace, or a DynamoDB counter table) — root CLAUDE.md says 'no new infra ... unless explicitly asked', so this task deliberately ships the weaker zero-infra version as a pragmatic first cut: it still stops a single client hammering a single instance, which is the common case for a low-traffic personal site.

tasks.design: These three shared components are each mounted from multiple add forms (TmdbSearch by both src/pages/reviews/movies/add.js and reviews/tv/add.js via the `mediaType` prop; BookSearch by reviews/books/add.js; LastfmAlbumSearch by reviews/albums/add.js AND vinyl/add.js), so fixing the shared components covers every add form in one task — no need to touch the `add.js` pages themselves.

tasks.title: Batch mark-done.sh / mark-reviewed.sh + their dashboard bulk endpoints into one commit per bulk action
tasks.design: Extend the two CLI scripts to accept MULTIPLE ids and do their git plumbing (add/commit/push) exactly ONCE regardless of how many ids are passed, then make the dashboard's bulk HTTP handlers call the script once with the full id list instead of looping per id.
verify: Build a HERMETIC scratch-repo test (own temp dir, `git init`, NOT this checkout) that copies the real `.harness/mark-done.sh` + `.harness/loop.sh` in, seeds a minimal TASKS.json fixture with 2+ needs-human tasks, runs `NO_PUSH=1 .harness/mark-done.sh T1 T2` (fixture ids) once, and asserts exactly ONE new commit was created touching human-done.json, and that both ids ended up `done:true` in it.
Same hermetic pattern for mark-reviewed.sh against a reviews.json fixture.
Assert the existing single-id call form (`mark-done.sh T123`, `mark-done.sh --undo T123`) still works unchanged against the same fixture.

Assert an invalid id in a batch (nonexistent task id, or — for mark-done.sh only — a task whose gate isn't needs-human) aborts BEFORE any file write or commit: the overlay file content and `git log` HEAD are unchanged after the failed call.
`grep -n "runScript(" .harness/dashboard/server.js` shows the bulk handler calling `runScript` exactly once per POST, not inside a `for`/`.forEach` over `ids`.
npm run lint / npm test / npm run build all pass.

ReviewCard: show a second 'edited' date line when an item has been edited
tasks.design:
Add a new optional field `editedDate` (a `DD-MM-YYYY` string, same format/convention as the existing `date` field) that review items may carry. In the JSX block at lines 86-90 (`{item.date && (

Date: {item.date}

)}`), keep that line unchanged, and immediately after it add a second conditional line that only renders when `item.editedDate` is truthy: `{item.editedDate && (

Edited: {item.editedDate}

)}`. Do not change behavior when `editedDate` is absent — only the single existing `Date:` line renders, exactly as today.

tasks.title: Movies update API: stamp a new editedDate field on every edit
tasks.design: Keep the existing `originalDate`/`preservedDate` logic (lines 32-44) exactly as-is — the original `date` field must still be preserved untouched. In addition, compute `const editedDate = new Date().toLocaleDateString('en-GB').replace(/\//g, '-');` (same convention already used in `add.js`'s `dateString`) and add `editedDate` to the `reviewData` object built at lines 61-71, alongside the existing `date: preservedDate` line. Every successful call to this route represents an edit, so `editedDate` should be set unconditionally on every update (not just when other fields changed).

tasks.title: TV update API: stamp a new editedDate field on every edit
tasks.design: Identical fix to the movies update route, applied to the TV update route: keep the existing `originalDate`/`preservedDate` logic (lines 32-44) unchanged, compute `editedDate` via `new Date().toLocaleDateString('en-GB').replace(/\//g, '-')`, and add it to the `reviewData` object built at lines 61-71 alongside `date: preservedDate`.

tasks.title: Books update API: stamp a new editedDate field on every edit
tasks.design: Keep the existing `originalDate`/`preservedDate` logic (lines 33-46) unchanged, compute `editedDate` via `new Date().toLocaleDateString('en-GB').replace(/\//g, '-')`, and add it to the `reviewData` object built at lines 66-83 alongside `date: preservedDate`.

tasks.title: Albums update API: stamp a new editedDate field on every edit
tasks.design: Add `#editedDate = :editedDate` (or similar) to the `updateExpressionParts` array (line 30) alongside the existing `#rating = :rating` and `highlights = :highlights` parts, add the corresponding entry to `expressionAttributeNames` and `expressionAttributeValues` (computed via `new Date().toLocaleDateString('en-GB').replace(/\//g, '-')`), so every album edit stamps `editedDate` via the existing `UpdateCommand`.

tasks.title: Movies list: sort by editedDate when present, else date
tasks.design: In both the `date-desc` branch (lines 61-67) and the default 'most recent first' branch (lines 68-75), replace the raw `a.date`/`b.date` reads with `(a.editedDate || a.date)`/`(b.editedDate || b.date)` before the `.split('-').reverse().join('-')` parsing, so sorting prefers the edited date when a review has been edited and falls back to the original date otherwise.

tasks.title: TV list: sort by editedDate when present, else date
tasks.design: In both the `date-desc` branch (lines 60-66) and the default 'most recent first' branch (lines 67-74), replace the raw `a.date`/`b.date` reads with `(a.editedDate || a.date)`/`(b.editedDate || b.date)` before the `.split('-').reverse().join('-')` parsing, so sorting prefers the edited date when a review has been edited and falls back to the original date otherwise.
